articles.title: Don't trust large context windows
articles.summary: Large context windows are often touted as a key feature of large language models, but the reality is that they can be misleading. The author notes that even when advertised to have a much larger window than needed, modern agents will still fall into the "dumb zone" where attention drops off and performance degrades. This means that developers should be cautious about relying on these windows and instead opt for more manual approaches like creating named artifacts or using tools that auto-compact context.

articles.title: Pac-Man, but you're the ghost
The future of Siri, or: why private inference isn’t private enough
articles.summary: Apple's plans to deploy private inference in its Siri ecosystem, which involves using a combination of Google Gemini models and Apple's Private Cloud Compute system for private hosting, raises questions about the balance between convenience and data protection. The use of these systems will allow Apple to process user queries and evaluate private data from devices, potentially offering more useful answers than external language models like those used by Google. However, this also means that sensitive context information from all Apple apps will be stored on their servers, which raises concerns about data security and the potential for unauthorized access.

articles.title: Free SQL→ER diagram tool, runs in the browser, nothing uploaded

The tool supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite and SQL Server syntax and works with standard CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE DDL. It's completely free and open-source, with no account or sign-up required, making it a convenient solution for developers to quickly visualize their database schema.

The practical posture is American capacity with global open standards, emphasizing freedom over profit and control.
